# factory-pilot

> Autonomous improvement agent for the Claude Code Factory pipeline.

factory-pilot runs a continuous diagnose-think-act-report loop to keep the software factory healthy without human intervention. Written in Go, deployed as a single Kubernetes pod.

## What it does

- Collects system health from Postgres (papers, candidates), SQLite (build registry), Kubernetes (pod status), GitHub API (repo metrics), and an in-cluster llm-router.
- Sends the full status report to Claude Opus for root-cause analysis. Opus returns a severity-ranked list of issues with exact fix commands.
- Executes fixes: kubectl operations (verb-whitelisted), code patches via Claude Code CLI, prompt template rewrites, and build retries.
- Persists structured daily reports to Postgres and exposes them via HTTP API.

## Key properties

- Dry-run by default. Must pass --execute to apply fixes.
- kubectl commands are verb-whitelisted (no delete, no exec, no --force) and namespace-locked to the factory namespace.
- Max 10 fixes per cycle. Default cycle interval: 6 hours.
- No UI. Backend-only agent.
- Go 1.23, minimal dependencies. No frameworks, no ORM.

## Structure

- main.go: CLI entry point (run, diagnose, report, serve, version), improvement loop orchestration
- internal/config/config.go: Environment-based configuration with sensible defaults
- internal/diagnose/collector.go: Parallel health collection orchestrator
- internal/diagnose/postgres.go: Paper ingestion and candidate pipeline stats
- internal/diagnose/sqlite.go: Build registry stats and error pattern analysis
- internal/diagnose/k8s.go: Pod health via kubectl
- internal/diagnose/github.go: Repo metrics via GitHub API (go-github)
- internal/diagnose/router.go: LLM router request stats
- internal/think/thinker.go: Claude Opus analysis engine, prompt construction, JSON issue parsing
- internal/think/types.go: Issue struct, severity ordering
- internal/act/executor.go: Fix dispatcher routing by fix_type
- internal/act/kubectl.go: Safe kubectl runner with verb whitelist and blocked patterns
- internal/act/claude.go: Claude Code CLI runner for code and prompt fixes
- internal/act/retry.go: SQLite build retry operations (single, all-failed, recent)
- internal/report/report.go: Daily report generation, printing, persistence orchestration
- internal/report/db.go: Postgres CRUD for pilot_reports table
- internal/api/server.go: HTTP monitoring API
- deploy/deployment.yaml: K8s Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, Role, RoleBinding
- Dockerfile: Multi-stage build (golang:1.23-alpine -> alpine:3.20)

## API

- GET /health — liveness probe, returns {"status":"ok"}
- GET /status — live system status snapshot from all collectors
- GET /reports — last 30 daily reports from Postgres
- GET /reports/{date} — report by date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- GET /issues — current issues from latest report

## Configuration (env vars)

- POSTGRES_URL — Postgres connection string (default: postgres://localhost:5432/factory?sslmode=disable)
- FACTORY_DATA_DIR — SQLite build registry path (default: /data/factory)
- FACTORY_GIT_DIR — local repo clone root (default: /data/repos)
- GITHUB_TOKEN — GitHub PAT for repo stats
- GITHUB_USER — GitHub username (default: timholm)
- CLAUDE_BINARY — path to Claude CLI (default: claude)
- K8S_NAMESPACE — target namespace (default: factory)
- CYCLE_INTERVAL — loop interval (default: 6h)
- MAX_FIXES_PER_CYCLE — fix cap per cycle (default: 10)
- API_PORT — HTTP listen port (default: 8090)

## Detailed docs

- CLAUDE.md: design constraints and coding conventions for AI agents modifying this codebase
- AGENTS.md: agent coordination, data flow contracts, and extension guide
